reviews.title: DON'T BOOK DURING MARDIS GRAS!!
reviews.text:
Ok, so we booked this hotel before reading the reviews because this was the last hotel open with rooms.   Why did they have rooms open?  Because  -  YOU CAN'T GET TO YOUR HOTEL!

The cab dropped us off out front mid day and the parade was going.  They won't let you cross the parade until it's over.  So we sat there 3 hours outside with our luggage trying to figure out how to the hotel.   

Did the hotel email us about this?  NO!  How about being proactive and provide some information on this!?

One parade finally ended and we were able to get across when the police open the barriers.   Another one started up about 10 mins later.   We had to walk about a mile to get around the parade to get out later that evening.  

This could have easily been solved had the hotel gave us drop off information and how to get around the parade.   Very disappointing.  

Check in staff had no sympathy or offered no upgrade/discount.  

Don't stay here.  Find something else.

reviews.title: If you can avoid, please do at all cost!
reviews.positives: Being able to check out and go home!
reviews.negatives: I recently stayed here and the experience did not meet my expectations at all! I’ve stayed at Marriott hotels in the past and I’ve never had this experience. I was blown away by this one in particular & NOT IN A GOOD WAY! The property itself was very outdated and could use a serious renovation! From the room amenities to the overall appearance of the hotel. For the price, I expected a more modern and well-maintained experience. My sisters and I were not helped with our luggage and we had to wait 2 hours for extra towels in our room. The valet service was also extremely unorganized. There was a lot of confusion when dropping off and retrieving vehicles, very long wait times, and little communication from staff, which made the process unnecessarily stressful. On top of that, both check-in and check-out were frustrating experiences. There were so many hidden fees. They tried charging me $186 for parking stating that I parked two vehicles when I only stayed one night & parked one vehicle. Overall, the stay was disappointing and lacked the level of comfort I typically expect from a Marriott property!

reviews.title: Disappointing Experience – Would Not Recommend
reviews.positives: I like the location
reviews.negatives:
Disappointing Experience – Would Not Recommend

I rarely leave negative reviews, but my experience at the Sheraton New Orleans was incredibly disappointing. Upon check-in, the staff was unfriendly and dismissive. When I inquired about an unexpected charge, I was met with a rude look and no explanation. Later, I discovered I had been double charged, adding to the frustration.

The condition of the room was unacceptable—extremely dirty, with carpets so grimy I didn’t feel comfortable walking barefoot. The shower didn’t work properly, and the provided shampoo bottle was completely empty. There was also no mini fridge in the room, which was inconvenient.

For a hotel of this caliber and price point, I expected much better service and cleanliness. Unfortunately, this stay fell far below basic hospitality standards.

reviews.title: Loved the location, but HATED the Water Damage and Elevator Issues..
reviews.positives: Stayed at the Sheraton on Canal Street in New Orleans, and while the location is excellent — just steps from Bourbon Street, great bars, and plenty of restaurants
reviews.negatives:
The hotel itself is currently facing major issues that significantly impacted my stay.

There was extensive water damage throughout multiple floors, and I had to be moved twice because the rooms I was assigned were on floors affected by a water main break. Unfortunately, the front desk team didn’t seem aware of which areas were impacted. I was placed in rooms with strong odors, wet vacuums running nonstop in the hallways, and, in one case, I couldn’t even exit the elevator because construction equipment completely blocked the doorway.

Check-out was equally frustrating. The elevators went down, and none of the staff were aware of the outage. With no phone signal to contact the front desk, I waited long enough that I almost missed my Uber to the airport. I eventually found the freight elevator myself and had to exit through a back storage area to leave the building.  The beds were sunken spaces, not good mattress quality at all!

reviews.title: Overcharged and under delivered. Pick someone else.
reviews.positives: The property is conveniently located. It was selected as one of the two hotels for the event that I came to town to participate in. The bell service was excellent.
reviews.negatives:
Inappropriately charged my card for a price different than what was the booking price guaranteed all inclusive of expenses. That was bad enough, but they charge that amount while continuing to also place the original hold that was implemented resulting in double billing to my account. Contacting the team to try to get this resolved with a complete total, very frustrating run around. I was originally redirected to the national number. The national number told me to call the hotel and ask for the general manager. It’s required me to call back to the hotel at the number I originally called and was connected to National. When I followed the National operators instructions, however, it was just more runaround. It took seven tries contacting back to the main desk only to reach a department manager. She was more interested in interrupting me and making excuses for why this was justified and actually helping.  She clearly couldn’t understand why I felt it was inappropriate for Marriott to continue to have a hold placed on my account while simultaneously charging me for the room. They don’t seem to acknowledge that the hold is obtained at the time of check-in in return for the promise that it will be immediately removed and replaced with the actual charges owed. This is basically converting money because it is retain for their use and denied for the customers.

Aside from the frustration and run around that I received getting this far, her promise to remove the hole and correct the charges still is not implemented two hours after the promise was made. In other words, they still wrongfully keeping my funds. To get this far, I’ve already wasted $795 of my time, which is more than they were entitled to charge me in the first place.

By the time I was done talking to her my frustration understandably, was increased rather than decreased.

There were some reserved signs that said SZH and we thought since we paid we could park there, my car got booted and it cost me $90 to get it off. reviews.positives: Location and the bartenders
reviews.negatives: The manager at the front said he couldn’t find or reservations, I had the paperwork in my hands. He only offers us one night and told we would have to find somewhere else to stay, when he could see my reservation. I told him it was still some availability on booking.com he told me NO until my husband intervene and he then gave us that option. The waitress and waiter during our stay never offer us any service and we sat in the restaurant lobby for 3 hours watching football games. We had to get up and go to bar every time to be served. Thanks to the two men at the bar because they were amazing. We got came back from watching the parade we were greeted by a sweet African American young lady that gave us great customer service. reviews.title: Stay clear of this hotel... and keep an eye out for their joieful scam once you arrive
reviews.positives: Location was good, valet staff were excellent, table attendants at bar were also excellent and friendly. The Cafe Bignet connected to the hotel and they had live music, good coffee and cheap breakfast everyday
reviews.negatives: The check-in folks were not. Not helpful, not clear, defensive, and overall the experience was worsened by hidden fees when I arrived. I paid $25/day non-optional and surprise "experience fee" which returned $30 discount cards (in reality $5 because I had to pay the non-optional fee) to use for specific activities... For example, I dont like ghost tours, whiskey tours or any other city tour, but those were the only options. I already had our vacation agenda in place and did not need their "encouragement" to do their recommended activities through a webpage called joieful (you had to sign up in order to get the discounts back). It truly seemed like a scam - it should be reported. On top of this, the check-in staff member said I would get $15 off bar meals per day as part of this experience fee. What they did was group two separate days ($60 I paid per day), which should have been two $15 discounts, into a single ($120) to give me a single $15 discount. The check out person was very defensive and had an aggravated look and ready for my questioning, which I ultimately avoided. I think this was all a scam. Its really disappointing, despite having a great experience in the city itself. NOLA is always wonderful. I will return but will make sure to hunt for a bed and breakfast, small local hotel or other, and steer away from this Sheraton. The last day they had a waste water pipe break in the lobby and cut off the water at 10am (could you imagine the mess? the smell was bad). They had a guard each night to watch who took the elevators. I noticed he asked my wife and I for ID's (hotel room cards) and did not ask others who just passed by... the guy was also not in a good mood.

reviews.title: Won’t be back and I am a little jaded about staying at Sheraton again.

reviews.negatives: I travel with a pet and am happy to pay the pet fee to assure that the rooms are clean. I always bring my own sheets to cover the beds and furniture so that my dog does not leave pet hair and oder behind. My room smelled like stinky dog and urine. I was moved to a room that was only mildly better. What happens with the pet fees that are collected? Clearly, the Sheraton does not invest it in cleaning carpets.         The Starbucks manager should be asked to speak with the employees about the way they treat people. They want the tip up front but as soon as they get it, you are nothing to them. So rude….

reviews.negatives: I had booked a double bed and by the time we arrived they were out of double rooms. To try to make up for this they offered a complimentary rollaway bed. The first night they ran out of rollaway beds and so one of our guests (me) had to actually sleep on the floor. They tried to give us more covers to make it more comfortable but obviously this was not as comfortable as a bed. The second day we had to make sure we were calling to ask for a rollaway bed when one became available rather than them trying to fix the situation. It also wasn’t complimentary anymore even though the man who checked me in told me it would be since they ran out double rooms. I understand it was a very busy weekend but this put a damper on the stay.

reviews.title: My stay was good except the bar manager who put a real discriminatory damper on our stay.
reviews.positives: Most of the staff was pleasant and helpful.  The food was good and the bed was comfortable.
reviews.negatives: The Bar Manager was extremely rude. She ignored us at the bar talking to the bartender, then proceeded to assist a caucasian male although we had been waiting much longer.  When we went to the desk to complain another manager was assisting us.  She knew we were telling him what happened.  She started to argue with us trying to defend herself then threw up her hand at us and told him, "You take care of that" and walked off.  She was extremely rude, disrespectful and unprofessional. There should be no tolerance for rude, disrespectful and unprofessional employees. We did make a complaint with another manager the next day who said she would address the matter. I could tolerate the bathroom door not closing in a room by myself but not the detrimental treatment. People like that should not be working with the public.

reviews.title: Very good
reviews.positives: I loved the location
reviews.negatives:
The room was clean and cozy.
The halls and elevator needed better cleaning.
The 8th floor, where the pool was located, had a room right off the elevators that was not blocked off and looked like it was getting worked on, but not at that time. 
Also, for our valet, we ordered our car via text service provided to us, so we texted for our car (we were staying on the 18th floor) proceeded down and waited 10 minutes, when we still hadn't received our car my husband went to the stand and found no one had even left to retrieve our vehicle. 
When I booked the description, said a small fridge would be located in the room and we didn't have one. Anyone traveling with kids would understand why this would be needed for kids' snacks and drinks.
Lastly, I paid extra for breakfast and never received breakfast nor told me where a breakfast buffet would be at
